title: Sanity + Next.js Clean App
description: A clean Next.js + Sanity starter with visual editing, drag-and-drop page builder, live content updates, and AI-powered media support.
usefulLines: – an open-source React application that connects to your Sanity project’s hosted dataset. The Studio is configured locally and can then be deployed for content collaboration.
to set up your local environment and deploy Sanity Studio.
2. Run Studio and Next.js app locally
Open the Next.js app running locally in your browser on

You may want to start with some sample content and we've got you covered. Run this command from the root of your project to import the provided dataset (sample-data.tar.gz) into your Sanity project. This step is optional but can be helpful for getting started quickly.
summary: Sanity + Next.js Clean App is a Starter reference with verified Next.js codebase, App Router structure, CMS integration hints. Site form: content-site. Verdict: valid.

{
  "name": "frontend",
  "private": true,
  "scripts": {
    "prebuild": "sanity typegen generate",
    "build": "next build",
    "predev": "npm run sanity:typegen",
    "dev": "next dev",
    "lint": "eslint .",
    "lint:fix": "eslint . --fix",
    "sanity:typegen": "cd ../studio && sanity schema extract --enforce-required-fields --path ../sanity.schema.json && cd ../frontend && sanity typegen generate",
    "start": "next start",
    "type-check": "tsc --noEmit"
  },
  "dependencies": {
    "@sanity/client": "^7.20.0",
    "@sanity/image-url": "^1.2.0",
    "@sanity/uuid": "^3.0.2",
    "@tailwindcss/postcss": "^4.2.2",
    "@tailwindcss/typography": "^0.5.19",
    "@vercel/speed-insights": "^1.3.1",
    "autoprefixer": "^10.4.27",
    "date-fns": "^4.1.0",
    "next": "16.2.1",
    "next-sanity": "^12.1.6",
    "postcss": "^8.5.8",
    "react": "19.2.4",
    "react-dom": "19.2.4",
    "sanity": "^5.18.0",
    "sanity-image": "^1.1.0",
    "sonner": "^2.0.7",
    "tailwindcss": "^4.2.2"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@types/node": "^20.19.37",
    "@types/react": "19.2.14",
    "@types/react-dom": "19.2.3",
    "eslint": "^9.39.4",
    "eslint-config-next": "16.2.1",
    "typescript": "5.9.3"
  }
}

import {Suspense} from 'react' import Link from 'next/link' import {PortableText} from '@portabletext/react' import {AllPosts} from '@/app/components/Posts' import GetStartedCode from '@/app/components/GetStartedCode' import SideBySideIcons from '@/app/components/SideBySideIcons' import {settingsQuery} from '@/sanity/lib/queries' import {sanityFetch} from '@/sanity/lib/live' import {dataAttr} from '@/sanity/lib/utils' export default async function Page() { const {data: settings} = await sanityFetch({ query: settingsQuery, }) return ( <>
A starter template for

Sanity + Next.js

NEXT_PUBLIC_SANITY_PROJECT_ID="<paste your project here>" # Required - The ID of your Sanity project
NEXT_PUBLIC_SANITY_DATASET="production" # Required - The dataset of your Sanity project
NEXT_PUBLIC_SANITY_API_VERSION="2025-09-25" # Optional - The API version provided to the Sanity Client
NEXT_PUBLIC_SANITY_STUDIO_URL="" #Optional, defaults to http://localhost:3333
SANITY_API_READ_TOKEN="<paste your token here>" # Required - The read token for your Sanity project

{
  "name": "sanity-template-nextjs-clean",
  "description": "Next.js + Sanity: A Powerful Website Starter with Real-time Visual Editing",
  "keywords": [
    "Sanity",
    "Next.js",
    "Headless CMS",
    "Visual Editing",
    "Live Content API (LCAPI)",
    "Real-time"
  ],
  "homepage": "https://github.com/sanity-io/sanity-template-nextjs-clean#readme",
  "bugs": {
    "url": "https://github.com/sanity-io/sanity-template-nextjs-clean/issues"
  },
  "workspaces": [
    "studio",
    "frontend"
  ],
  "scripts": {
    "dev": "npx run-p --print-label dev:*",
    "dev:next": "npm run dev --workspace=frontend",
    "dev:studio": "npm run dev --workspace=studio",
    "format": "prettier --cache --write .",
    "import-sample-data": "cd studio && sanity dataset import sample-data.tar.gz --dataset production --replace",
    "lint": "npm run lint --workspace=frontend",
    "type-check": "npm run type-check --workspaces"
  },
  "prettier": "@sanity/prettier-config",
  "devDependencies": {
    "@sanity/prettier-config": "^2.0.3",
    "npm-run-all2": "^5.0.2",
    "prettier": "^3.8.1"
  }
}

styleRules: Keep the strongest layout rhythm from the reference, but adapt it to the chosen runtime scaffold.
Prefer polished, production-looking App Router structure over screenshot-faithful clones.
Treat starter/boilerplate references as structural baselines, not final visual direction.
Prioritize content scanning, navigation clarity, and readable documentation rhythm.
avoidPatterns: Do not paste large reference code blocks verbatim into the generated site.
Do not let reference aesthetics override the user's actual topic, language, or route plan.
Avoid shipping starter-like visual defaults (generic cards, weak hierarchy, placeholder-feel copy).
Avoid forcing documentation references into pricing-first SaaS structures.
worldClassRubric: Professional hierarchy above the fold: strong headline, CTA clarity, and balanced spacing.
Reusable sections with believable content, not generic lorem-style filler.
Accessible semantics and responsive layout decisions that survive a real Next.js preview.
Final output should feel custom and production-grade, not like a renamed starter.
Content-first sites should feel structured, findable, and stable.
